name: Filled Berlin Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Use up to 4 cups of Unbleached Flour in this recipe.
2. Heat the fat to 375 degrees F. for frying the doughnuts.
3. Soften the yeast in the warm water and let stand for 5 minutes, or until it "blooms" or proofs.
4. In a large bowl, combine sugar, salt, butter, orange juice, and rum extract. Add scalded milk and stir until butter is melted. Cool to lukewarm.
5. When cool, blend in 1 cup of unbleached flour and beat until smooth. Stir in the yeast and add about half of the remaining flour, beating until smooth. Beat in eggs. Then beat in enough of the remaining flour to make a soft dough (slightly sticky and light in weight).
6. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured board and let rest for 5-10 minutes.
7. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 8-10 minutes). Form into a ball and place in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top of the dough. Cover and let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in bulk.
8. Punch down the dough, kneading lightly to remove air pockets. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Roll to 1/2-inch thickness.
9. Cut rounds using a 3-inch biscuit or cookie cutter. Let rise on the rolling surface away from drafts and direct heat until doubled in bulk (30-45 minutes).
10. About 20 minutes before frying, heat the fat.
11. Fry doughnuts in the hot fat, adding only as many as will float in a single layer without crowding. Cook for 2-3 minutes or until lightly browned; turn with a fork or tongs to prevent piercing and ensure they rise to the surface.
12. Lift from the fat, draining briefly before removing to absorbent paper towels.
13. Cool. Cut a slit through the center of each doughnut. Fill with about 1/2 teaspoon jam or jelly using a pastry bag and tube. Press lightly to close the slit.
14. Shake the pastry bag with 2-3 teaspoons granulated or powdered sugar (your choice) before filling, shaking gently to remove excess sugar.
